author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Implemented v1 → v2 nomenclature rename across the codebase
(commit `20efe38`) AND fixed a regression discovered when running
the migration against live planner data (commit `6184019`).

The rename: storage types, CLI surface, wire format, on-disk
paths, and the `bugs` → `issues` bookmark all migrated. Trailer
parser tolerates both `Jjf-Bug:` (v1) and `Jjf-Issue:` (v2)
on read; writer emits only v2.

The regression: the inline `Storage::maybe_migrate_v1_to_v2`
correctly renamed paths and the bookmark, but two defects made
it unreachable AND broken even when reachable:

1. CLI preflight `issues_bookmark` ran before `Storage::open`,
   so on a real v1 repo it errored with `MissingIssuesBookmark`
   and the migration never fired. Fix: when `issues` is absent
   AND `bugs` is present, the preflight now calls
   `Storage::open` to trigger the migration, then proceeds.

2. After migration renamed `bugs/<id>.*` to `issues/<id>.*`,
   the history walker and read-replay filtered ancestor commits
   on `root:issues/<id>.*` ONLY. Pre-migration commits dropped
   out of the filter — including each issue's `create` op
   stanza. `read()` then failed with "no `create` op found in
   chain." Fix: include both v1 and v2 paths in the filter.
   New crate-internal `v1_issue_json_relpath` /
   `v1_issue_comments_relpath` helpers; both call sites
   (`history.rs::read_history_at`, `read.rs::replay_ops`)
   updated.

# Recommendation

Close. 189/189 workspace tests green. Live planner data (10
issues on the now-renamed `issues` bookmark) verified readable
end-to-end via `jjf ls` and `jjf show`. The bookmark migration
ran successfully on the live data during this round; no
operator action needed.

# Confidence

High on both the rename mechanics and the regression fix. New
integration test `v1_to_v2_migration_preserves_history`
synthesizes a v1 repo from a known-good v2 state (renames
bookmark + paths back to v1 shape), drives `Storage::open` +
`read` + `read_history`, and asserts the create op survives
the migration via the v1 path filter. This is the test that
was missing from the original rename round.

# Open follow-ups

- Same-second collision (spec §5.6) — the underlying root
  cause of two tests needing 1.1s sleeps. Hardened in this
  round; the real fix is moving `updated_at` to
  nano-resolution (spec §3.1 currently mandates second
  resolution). v3 spec conversation.
- `subagent-working-a-git-bug-issue` skill is post-cutover
  stale; rewrite tracked under `epic:agent-ergonomics`
  (`5a755ec` / `ergo-subagent-skill`).
- Downstream ticket `7100b51` (`type` + `slug` fields) is
  unblocked. Trailer parser is extension-tolerant per spec
  §5.2; adding new field-bearing ops is non-breaking for
  existing trailers.
